detail.title: Agent Rename, Scope Tightening, and New Blind Comparator

produces.artifact: scripts/lib/eval-graders.js with loadAgentDef paths updated to match

requirement.title: Rename eval-comparator to eval-analyzer
requirement.description: The prompt template file currently at skills/arc-evaluating/agents/eval-comparator.md (post fr-fs-001 migration) MUST be renamed to eval-analyzer.md. Every loadAgentDef call site in scripts/lib/ and every documentation reference in skills/arc-evaluating/SKILL.md + references/* MUST be updated to the new name. The old name MUST NOT remain as a symlink, alias, or stub — renaming is total.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: After rename, skills/arc-evaluating/agents/eval-analyzer.md MUST exist and skills/arc-evaluating/agents/eval-comparator.md MUST NOT exist. fr-ag-001

After rename, grep across the repository MUST return zero matches for "eval-comparator" except in (a) CHANGELOG.md migration notes and (b) historical benchmark JSON files under evals/benchmarks/. fr-ag-001
arc eval compare MUST run end-to-end after rename without ENOENT or file-not-found errors related to the agent prompt. fr-ag-001

requirement.title: Strip Verdict Authority From eval-analyzer
requirement.description: The eval-analyzer prompt template MUST NOT instruct the subagent to emit verdicts of the form SHIP, NEEDS_WORK, BLOCKED, RUN_MORE_TRIALS, INVESTIGATE, or INSUFFICIENT_DATA. Its scope is post-hoc analysis only: why a delta appeared, which assertions are non-discriminative across trials, variance hotspots, and qualitative observations. The verdict decision MUST be computed deterministically by the harness from pass rate and k (see fr-vr-001). If the agent's output accidentally contains a recommendation field, the CLI MUST ignore it — verdict provenance is harness, not agent.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The eval-analyzer.md prompt template MUST NOT contain instructions asking the subagent to emit a verdict, recommendation, or SHIP-class decision. A grep of the prompt text for "SHIP", "NEEDS_WORK", "BLOCKED", "RUN_MORE", "INVESTIGATE", "INSUFFICIENT_DATA" as verdict tokens MUST return zero matches. fr-ag-002

The arc eval compare CLI MUST obtain the verdict from the deterministic harness computation. Any recommendation field appearing in the agent response MUST be dropped or logged as a warning; it MUST NOT influence the final verdict surfaced to the user. fr-ag-002
eval-analyzer output structure MUST include fields for delta_explanation, weak_assertions_patterns, and variance_notes — analytical content, not decisions. fr-ag-002

requirement.title: Introduce eval-blind-comparator Prompt Template
requirement.description: A new prompt template eval-blind-comparator.md MUST be created at skills/arc-evaluating/agents/eval-blind-comparator.md. It is loaded by the harness via loadAgentDef and spawned via execCommand('claude', ...) — same pattern as eval-grader and eval-analyzer. The template MUST instruct the subagent to receive two anonymized outputs labeled A and B plus the original prompt, generate a task-derived rubric, score each output, and return winner / tie with structured reasoning. The harness MUST shuffle baseline/treatment labels randomly per comparison pair and restore the mapping after the agent responds. The template is invoked per fr-gr-005 only when all assertions are model-graded.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: skills/arc-evaluating/agents/eval-blind-comparator.md MUST exist and contain a prompt template that, when fed two outputs plus a prompt, yields a JSON-parseable response matching the blind comparator schema (winner, reasoning, rubric). fr-ag-003
The harness MUST randomize A/B label assignment per pair — across many comparisons, the mapping from (baseline, treatment) to (A, B) MUST be approximately uniform. fr-ag-003
The prompt sent to the blind-comparator subagent MUST NOT contain the strings "baseline", "treatment", "with_skill", "without_skill", or the skill name anywhere in the comparison payload. fr-ag-003
